directions.name: Trim the hot-band export path by batching 64x64 stores vertically instead of horizontally
directions.hypothesis: The dominant hot-band kernel is back on the best known surface and recent copy / consumer experiments have mostly been negative. The current 64x64 export helper still pays one `__syncwarp()` per row and per column pair, which means eight warp synchronizations to export the sixteen 16x16 accumulator tiles. Because each warp only has two `c_shared` scratch stages, a practical next move is to flip the batching dimension: use those two stages to store two rows for the same column tile, then export them together. That preserves shared footprint and accumulator layout while potentially cutting the export-side warp sync count in half.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band epilogue / export synchronization and shared-memory round-trip overhead.
directions.risk: Moderate. The change is local and footprint-neutral, but the export path is correctness-sensitive and the accumulator-to-output mapping must stay exact.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if correctness breaks, if registers rise materially, or if the hot-band time stays flat.

directions.name: Re-anchor explicitly at the current best custom commit before more export work
directions.hypothesis: The measured best is now `0d78758`, and if the export rewrite becomes noisy or hard to validate, the safer option is to re-anchor the loop at that exact measured commit instead of staying on the latest recovery surface.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Workflow / baseline drift rather than a micro-bottleneck.
directions.risk: Low. This does not advance performance by itself, but it keeps the loop grounded on the strongest measured branch.
directions.stop_condition: Use this if the export experiment fails quickly or if runtime drift becomes confusing.

directions.name: Revisit stage peeling only after export behavior is simpler
directions.hypothesis: The last steady-state peeling attempt failed correctness, but part of that may be because the hot-band loop and export path are both still cluttered. If the export path becomes simpler and more deterministic, a later steady-state rewrite becomes easier to reason about. It should not be the immediate next move, but it remains a follow-up once the epilogue side is trimmed.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Fixed-shape control and epilogue interaction after export simplification.
directions.risk: High. The earlier correctness failure is still recent, so this should only come back after a simpler export path is in place.
directions.stop_condition: Do not spend a round here until the export rewrite has been resolved.

notes: Round 1/50 starts from the current best custom measurement at 29.325824 ms. The hot-band kernel still dominates and recent copy / consumer changes mostly regressed, so the next defensible move is to attack the export side. Recommended direction dir_01 keeps the same accumulator layout and shared footprint but changes the 64x64 export batching from horizontal pairs to vertical pairs, which should reduce warp-sync count in the hot-band epilogue. Dir_02 is a strict best-commit re-anchor fallback, and dir_03 records that fixed-shape peeling should only be revisited after the export behavior becomes simpler.
